user_input: What is the purpose of integrating Basic Engineering Mathematics into the BSECE program?
retrieved_contexts:
['The BSECE program, with Ignatian ideals, emphasizes core competencies that are essential for ECE
Braduates. These competencies reflect technical skills and Ignatian values such as problem-solving, design skills,
critical thinking, and more. To ensure that these competencies are attained, it is essential to strengthen foundational
Subjects like mathematics. This will be achieved by integrating Basic Engineering Mathematics into the program.

Adding Basic Engineering Mathematics will allow STEM and non-STEM SHS to Join the program. As an option,
emerging technology professional electives are included with the demand for new technology. The new professional
electives courses will supplement the senior’s projects. In addition to the revision, the course code for Differential
Equation and Engineering Technopreneurship was changed to align with other engineering programs.

a. Program Title and Description', 'a. Program Title and Description

The Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering (BSECE) program is structured to cultivate engineers with a
robust grounding in mathematics, natural sciences, physical sciences, and related disciplines. Accordingly, the
program comprises mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals courses, focusing on nurturing analytical
and creative skills. It also encompasses language studies, social sciences, humanities, and other core courses to
ensure that graduates are articulate and comprehend the significance of their roles in society and the impact of their
work on societal advancement.', 'Ateneo de Naga University
College of Science, Engineering, and Architecture
Electronics and Computer Engineering Department

12 and CMO 37 s. 2012, this curriculum implements the shift to outcomes-based education (OBE), leading to
Competency-based standards. This includes the “core competencies” expected of Bachelor of Science in Electronics
Engineering (BSECE) graduates with Ignatian Ideals.', 'The program equips students with essential engineering tools to tackte challenges in electronics engineering,
enabling them to pursue diverse career opportunities. Electives are included to facilitate specialization, enabling
students to develop expertise in particular areas. The program also emphasizes fundamental concepts, updating
existing courses to reflect new developments.

user_input: What disciplines are the Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering program grounded in?
retrieved_contexts:
['a. Program Title and Description

The Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering (BSECE) program is structured to cultivate engineers with a
robust grounding in mathematics, natural sciences, physical sciences, and related disciplines. Accordingly, the
program comprises mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals courses, focusing on nurturing analytical
and creative skills. It also encompasses language studies, social sciences, humanities, and other core courses to
ensure that graduates are articulate and comprehend the significance of their roles in society and the impact of their
work on societal advancement.', 'Ateneo de Naga University
College of Science, Engineering, and Architecture
Electronics and Computer Engineering Department
Revised Curriculum for Bachelor of Science In Electronics Engineering (BSECE) S/Y 2024-2025
Based on CMO No. 101 Serles of 2017
I. Rationale ,

20 With the integration of outcomes-based, according to the guidelines for implementing CMO No. 46 series of

12 and CMO 37 s. 2012, this curriculum implements the shift to outcomes-based education (OBE), leading to
Competency-based standards. This includes the “core competencies” expected of Bachelor of Science in Electronics
Engineering (BSECE) graduates with Ignatian Ideals.', 'The program equips students with essential engineering tools to tackte challenges in electronics engineering,
enabling them to pursue diverse career opportunities. Electives are included to facilitate specialization, enabling
students to develop expertise in particular areas. The program also emphasizes fundamental concepts, updating
existing courses to reflect new developments.

1', 'The BSECE program, with Ignatian ideals, emphasizes core competencies that are essential for ECE
Braduates. These competencies reflect technical skills and Ignatian values such as problem-solving, design skills,
critical thinking, and more. To ensure that these competencies are attained, it is essential to strengthen foundational
Subjects like mathematics. This will be achieved by integrating Basic Engineering Mathematics into the program.

Adding Basic Engineering Mathematics will allow STEM and non-STEM SHS to Join the program. As an option,
emerging technology professional electives are included with the demand for new technology. The new professional
electives courses will supplement the senior’s projects. In addition to the revision, the course code for Differential
Equation and Engineering Technopreneurship was changed to align with other engineering programs.

In a Lecture Course or Non-Laboratory Course, the grading components are divided as follows:
| Grading Components | Percentage of Converted Final Raw Score (CFRS) |
| --- | --- |
| Class Standing | 33.33% |
| Preliminary Examination | 33.33% |
| Pre-Final Examination + Comprehensive Final Examination | 33.33% |

Since the Pre-Final Examination and Comprehensive Final Examination together account for 33.33% of the grading components, and there is no separate percentage given for the Comprehensive Final Examination, we can assume that the Comprehensive Final Examination is a part of the 33.33% allocated to the Pre-Final Examination + Comprehensive Final Examination. However, the exact percentage allocated to the Comprehensive Final Examination is not specified.
